rules.label: 17.A.
rules.html: Unless specified differently elsewhere, an infraction may only be called by a <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn> on the infracted team who recognizes that it has occurred. The player must know that a specific rule was violated and have perceived the particular action with certainty. The player must immediately call “<dfn data-rule="3.P">violation</dfn>” or the name of the specific infraction loudly.
rules.text: Unless specified differently elsewhere, an infraction may only be called by a player on the infracted team who recognizes that it has occurred. The player must know that a specific rule was violated and have perceived the particular action with certainty. The player must immediately call “violation” or the name of the specific infraction loudly.
annotations: A player may not call an infraction whenever the player merely suspects that an infraction might have occurred.

rules.label: 17.B.
rules.html: A <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn> called for an infraction may contest that call if that player believes the infraction did not occur.
rules.text: A player called for an infraction may contest that call if that player believes the infraction did not occur.
annotations: This belief may be based on the player’s perspective on the particular sequence of events or based on a disagreement over the application of the rules, provided the player has read and understands the rules. For example, “No, I didn’t slap your hand” or “Tipping the disc to someone else is not a travel.”

rules.label: 17.C.
rules.html: Any time an infraction is called, the Continuation Rule applies. Continuation Rule: Play stops when the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> in possession acknowledges that an infraction has been called. If a call is made when there is no thrower in <dfn data-rule="3.I">possession of the disc</dfn> and the disc is not in the air (for example, the disc is lying on the ground), play stops at the time of the call. However, if a call is made when the disc is in the air or the thrower is in the act of throwing, or if the thrower fails to acknowledge the call and subsequently attempts a pass, play continues until the outcome of that pass is determined. For the purpose of the Continuation Rule, an uncontested stall that occurs after another call is treated the same as an incomplete pass. Play then either stops or continues according to the following:
rules.text: Any time an infraction is called, the Continuation Rule applies. Continuation Rule: Play stops when the thrower in possession acknowledges that an infraction has been called. If a call is made when there is no thrower in possession of the disc and the disc is not in the air (for example, the disc is lying on the ground), play stops at the time of the call. However, if a call is made when the disc is in the air or the thrower is in the act of throwing, or if the thrower fails to acknowledge the call and subsequently attempts a pass, play continues until the outcome of that pass is determined. For the purpose of the Continuation Rule, an uncontested stall that occurs after another call is treated the same as an incomplete pass. Play then either stops or continues according to the following:
annotations: Thus, if you get stalled before you acknowledge a call, it is treated the same as if you ignored the call and threw a turnover.

children.label: 17.C.1.
children.html: Despite any outcome dictated by these rules, if the involved players on both teams agree that the infraction did not affect the outcome of the play, play stops and the result of the play stands. This provision does not apply if the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> is aware an infraction has been called and subsequently attempts a pass.
children.text: Despite any outcome dictated by these rules, if the involved players on both teams agree that the infraction did not affect the outcome of the play, play stops and the result of the play stands. This provision does not apply if the thrower is aware an infraction has been called and subsequently attempts a pass.

children.label: 17.C.2.
children.html: Where the outcome of these rules is for play to continue unhalted, the <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn> who called the infraction must announce “play on.” Additionally, any player recognizing that play should continue without stoppage should announce “play on.” If the player who called the infraction does not announce “play on” and the opposing team is uncertain whether play should continue, the opposing team has the option to stop play by calling “<dfn data-rule="3.P">violation</dfn>.”
children.text: Where the outcome of these rules is for play to continue unhalted, the player who called the infraction must announce “play on.” Additionally, any player recognizing that play should continue without stoppage should announce “play on.” If the player who called the infraction does not announce “play on” and the opposing team is uncertain whether play should continue, the opposing team has the option to stop play by calling “violation.”

children.label: 17.C.3.
children.html: For calls made by the offense:
children.text: For calls made by the offense:

children.label: 17.C.3.a.
children.html: If the pass is complete:
children.text: If the pass is complete:

children.label: 17.C.3.a.1.
children.html: If the offense called the infraction before the thrower began the act of throwing (3.N.4), play stops and possession reverts to the thrower.
children.text: If the offense called the infraction before the thrower began the act of throwing (3.N.4), play stops and possession reverts to the thrower.

children.label: 17.C.3.a.2.
children.html: If the offense called the infraction after the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> began the act of throwing, play continues unhalted.
children.text: If the offense called the infraction after the thrower began the act of throwing, play continues unhalted.

children.label: 17.C.3.a.3.
children.html: Where an infraction is called by the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn>, players should treat the call as if it was made when the infraction occurred in determining whether the call was made before or after the thrower was in the act of throwing. If the infraction occurred after the <dfn data-rule="3.N">throw</dfn> was released, the call is considered to have been made by a non-thrower.
children.text: Where an infraction is called by the thrower, players should treat the call as if it was made when the infraction occurred in determining whether the call was made before or after the thrower was in the act of throwing. If the infraction occurred after the throw was released, the call is considered to have been made by a non-thrower.
annotations: This means that if you throw a turnover and call a foul on contact after the release, the turnover will generally stand. Note that the timing is based on when the infraction occurred for this rule.

children.label: 17.C.3.b.
children.html: If the pass is incomplete:
children.text: If the pass is incomplete:

children.label: 17.C.3.b.1.
children.html: If the infraction affected the play (17.C.5), play stops and the disc reverts to the thrower at the time the infraction occurred unless the specific rule (e.g., the receiving foul rule) says otherwise.
children.text: If the infraction affected the play (17.C.5), play stops and the disc reverts to the thrower at the time the infraction occurred unless the specific rule (e.g., the receiving foul rule) says otherwise.

children.label: 17.C.3.b.2.
children.html: If the infraction did not affect the play, play stops and the result of the play stands.
children.text: If the infraction did not affect the play, play stops and the result of the play stands.

children.label: 17.C.3.b.3.
children.html: Where the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> calls an infraction which occurred before the thrower began the act of throwing, play continues unhalted and the result of the play stands.
children.text: Where the thrower calls an infraction which occurred before the thrower began the act of throwing, play continues unhalted and the result of the play stands.

children.label: 17.C.4.
children.html: For calls made by the defense:
children.text: For calls made by the defense:

children.label: 17.C.4.a.
children.html: If the pass is complete:
children.text: If the pass is complete:

children.label: 17.C.4.a.1.
children.html: If the infraction affected the play (17.C.5), play stops and the disc reverts to the thrower at the time the infraction occurred unless the specific rule (e.g., the receiving foul rule) says otherwise.
children.text: If the infraction affected the play (17.C.5), play stops and the disc reverts to the thrower at the time the infraction occurred unless the specific rule (e.g., the receiving foul rule) says otherwise.

children.label: 17.C.4.a.2.
children.html: If the infraction did not affect the play, play stops and the result of the play stands.
children.text: If the infraction did not affect the play, play stops and the result of the play stands.

children.label: 17.C.4.b.
children.html: If the pass is incomplete, play continues un-halted.
children.text: If the pass is incomplete, play continues un-halted.

children.label: 17.C.5.
children.html: An infraction affected the play if an infracted <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn> determines that the outcome of the specific play, from the time of the infraction until play stops, may have been meaningfully different absent the infraction. For example, if a receiver is fouled and thereby prevented from getting open for a pass, the play was affected; however, if the receiver would not have received a pass even without the <dfn data-rule="3.B">foul</dfn>, the play was not affected.
children.text: An infraction affected the play if an infracted player determines that the outcome of the specific play, from the time of the infraction until play stops, may have been meaningfully different absent the infraction. For example, if a receiver is fouled and thereby prevented from getting open for a pass, the play was affected; however, if the receiver would not have received a pass even without the foul, the play was not affected.
annotations: It is the infracted player’s responsibility to announce if play was affected. For example, if a defender calls “pick” while trailing a receiver by 6 feet, the defender should indicate whether the pick affected the play. If the pick did not affect the play, the defender will still recover any distance lost, but the completed pass will stand. If the pick did affect the play, the disc reverts to the thrower.

Contact that occurs after the outcome of the play is determined cannot affect the play. For example, if a defender catches a disc before lightly bumping into the receiver, that contact did not affect the play and the turnover will stand.

children.label: 17.C.6.
children.html: Positioning after a call:
children.text: Positioning after a call:

children.label: 17.C.6.a.
children.html: If no pass is thrown or attempted before the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> stops play by acknowledging the call, all players return to the locations they occupied when the call was made.
children.text: If no pass is thrown or attempted before the thrower stops play by acknowledging the call, all players return to the locations they occupied when the call was made.

children.label: 17.C.6.b.
children.html: If a pass is thrown or attempted before the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> acknowledges the call or the call is made after the throwing attempt, and
children.text: If a pass is thrown or attempted before the thrower acknowledges the call or the call is made after the throwing attempt, and

children.label: 17.C.6.b.1.
children.html: if possession reverts to the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn>, all players return to the locations they occupied at the earlier of:
children.text: if possession reverts to the thrower, all players return to the locations they occupied at the earlier of:
annotations: Therefore, if a long pass is thrown and there is a contested receiving foul, the players will return to where they were at the time of the throw (rather than remaining downfield after chasing down the disc).

children.label: 17.C.6.b.1.a.
children.html: the time of the <dfn data-rule="3.N">throw</dfn>,
children.text: the time of the throw,

children.label: 17.C.6.b.1.b.
children.html: the time of the call.
children.text: the time of the call.

children.label: 17.C.6.b.2.
children.html: if a <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn> other than the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> is awarded possession due to an uncontested call, all players return to the locations they occupied at the time of the infraction.
children.text: if a player other than the thrower is awarded possession due to an uncontested call, all players return to the locations they occupied at the time of the infraction.

children.label: 17.C.6.b.3.
children.html: if the result of a play stands, players return to the locations they occupied when play stopped. In addition, an infracted <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn> is then allowed to move to recover the relative position lost because of the infraction.
children.text: if the result of a play stands, players return to the locations they occupied when play stopped. In addition, an infracted player is then allowed to move to recover the relative position lost because of the infraction.

rules.label: 17.D.
rules.html: When a call is made, throwers must stop play by visibly or audibly communicating the stoppage as soon as they are aware of the call.
rules.text: When a call is made, throwers must stop play by visibly or audibly communicating the stoppage as soon as they are aware of the call.
annotations: Players should echo calls on the field.

If the marker has made reasonable efforts to notify the thrower of a call and believes that the thrower has not stopped play promptly enough, the marker may call a violation under this rule and a completed pass will come back to the thrower.

rules.label: 17.E.
rules.html: If it is unclear whether a catch was made before the disc contacted the ground, or whether a <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn>’s first point of <dfn data-rule="3.C">ground contact</dfn> after catching the disc was in- or out-of-bounds or in or out of the end zone, the player with the <dfn data-rule="3.A">best perspective</dfn> makes the call.
rules.text: If it is unclear whether a catch was made before the disc contacted the ground, or whether a player’s first point of ground contact after catching the disc was in- or out-of-bounds or in or out of the end zone, the player with the best perspective makes the call.

rules.label: 17.F.
rules.html: If a player makes a play-halting call and subsequently determines that their call was incorrect, they should retract the call by announcing “retracted.” Play still stops. Play restarts with a check and the stall resumes according to 15.F.1.
rules.text: If a player makes a play-halting call and subsequently determines that their call was incorrect, they should retract the call by announcing “retracted.” Play still stops. Play restarts with a check and the stall resumes according to 15.F.1.
annotations: This includes a player incorrectly stopping play because they thought they heard a call such as “pick” and echoed the call.

rules.label: 17.G.
rules.html: If a dispute arises concerning an infraction or the outcome of a play (e.g., a catch where no one had a good perspective), and the teams cannot come to a satisfactory resolution, play stops, and the disc is returned to the thrower and put into play with a check (10.D.3), with the count reached plus one or at six if over five.
rules.text: If a dispute arises concerning an infraction or the outcome of a play (e.g., a catch where no one had a good perspective), and the teams cannot come to a satisfactory resolution, play stops, and the disc is returned to the thrower and put into play with a check (10.D.3), with the count reached plus one or at six if over five.

rules.label: 17.H.
rules.html: If an infraction results in possession reverting to a <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> who was airborne when releasing the disc, play restarts at the spot on the playing field closest to the point of release.
rules.text: If an infraction results in possession reverting to a thrower who was airborne when releasing the disc, play restarts at the spot on the playing field closest to the point of release.
annotations: This applies to throwers attempting a “greatest” or any other airborne thrower.

rules.label: 17.I.
rules.html: If multiple calls occur on the same play or before play stops, the outcomes should be resolved in reverse sequence (latest call first, earliest call last).
rules.text: If multiple calls occur on the same play or before play stops, the outcomes should be resolved in reverse sequence (latest call first, earliest call last).
annotations: Examples: A receiver and their opponent contact each other and both call fouls against the other. 17.I.2 applies, and the disc is returned to the thrower and put into play with a check, with the count reached plus one or at six if over five. Two different receiver-opponent pairs call fouls — one against the offense and one against the defense — while the disc is in the air, but before anyone starts trying to catch it. 17.I.3 applies in this case, even if the fouls occur several seconds apart. The disc is returned to the thrower and put into play with a check, with the count reached plus one or at six if over five. The marker calls an offensive foul on the thrower during the throw, then later a receiver calls a defensive foul on a defender during the reception on an incomplete pass. The fouls are resolved in reverse sequence. First, the receiving foul is resolved (here, assumed uncontested), granting possession to the offensive receiver, then the throwing foul is resolved (here, assumed uncontested), returning the disc to the thrower with the count reached plus one (max nine). The earlier infraction (offensive foul on the thrower) takes precedence over the later infraction (defensive foul on the receiver), resulting in the disc returning to the original thrower.

children.label: 17.I.1.
children.html: If an earlier infraction is determined not to have affected the play (17.C.1 and 17.C.5), or is retracted (17.F), that infraction is not resolved and it does not affect the resolution of subsequent infractions.
children.text: If an earlier infraction is determined not to have affected the play (17.C.1 and 17.C.5), or is retracted (17.F), that infraction is not resolved and it does not affect the resolution of subsequent infractions.
annotations: Not resolved essentially means that the call is treated as if it had not occurred and the result of the play (possession, stall count, etc.) is unchanged by that call.

children.label: 17.I.2.
children.html: Offsetting infractions: If both offensive and defensive infractions occur simultaneously or the sequence cannot be determined, the resolution for this set of infractions is: the disc is returned to the <dfn data-rule="3.O">thrower</dfn> and put into play with a check, with the count reached plus one or at six if over five.
children.text: Offsetting infractions: If both offensive and defensive infractions occur simultaneously or the sequence cannot be determined, the resolution for this set of infractions is: the disc is returned to the thrower and put into play with a check, with the count reached plus one or at six if over five.
annotations: It is possible that resolutions to earlier infractions may ultimately change this particular outcome.

children.label: 17.I.3.
children.html: Exception: If separate fouls occur while the disc is in the air, but before any <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn> starts to attempt to catch or block the disc, those fouls are treated as simultaneous and on the same play.
children.text: Exception: If separate fouls occur while the disc is in the air, but before any player starts to attempt to catch or block the disc, those fouls are treated as simultaneous and on the same play.

rules.label: 17.J.
rules.html: A <dfn data-rule="3.Q.10">player</dfn>’s ability to catch or make a play on the disc is not considered to be “affected” because that player stopped, slowed down, or otherwise ceased to continue playing because a call was made by another player. Players are encouraged to make every effort to continue playing until play actually stops.
rules.text: A player’s ability to catch or make a play on the disc is not considered to be “affected” because that player stopped, slowed down, or otherwise ceased to continue playing because a call was made by another player. Players are encouraged to make every effort to continue playing until play actually stops.
